title: AI Agent Autonomy Level Frameworks Comparison
description: Three major frameworks for classifying AI agent autonomy, aligned by level

frameworks.name: Knight Columbia
frameworks.source: Knight First Amendment Institute at Columbia University

levels.knight_columbia: No AI — human performs all tasks
levels.health_example: Clinician reads X-ray without any AI assistance

levels.knight_columbia: AI generates content, human decides and executes
levels.data_agents: AI assists with subtasks on request
levels.bessemer: AI suggests, human decides and acts
levels.health_example: AI highlights suspicious region, radiologist decides whether to investigate

levels.knight_columbia: AI executes with human approval required before action
levels.data_agents: AI executes routine tasks, human monitors
levels.bessemer: AI drafts output, human approves before delivery
levels.health_example: AI drafts radiology report, radiologist reviews and signs off

levels.knight_columbia: AI executes autonomously, human reviews after the fact
levels.data_agents: AI handles full workflow with human oversight
levels.bessemer: AI executes tasks, human spot-checks periodically
levels.health_example: AI triages emergency cases and routes to specialists, clinician reviews queue

levels.knight_columbia: AI autonomous within defined boundaries, human sets goals
levels.data_agents: AI autonomous with guardrails and escalation policies
levels.bessemer: AI fully autonomous, human handles exceptions only
levels.health_example: AI manages routine screening program (e.g., IDx-DR), escalates abnormals to ophthalmologist

levels.knight_columbia: Full autonomy — AI sets own goals and executes
levels.health_example: Hypothetical: AI manages entire clinical pathway end-to-end (not yet deployed in healthcare)
